trending_skills.name: Inference Optimizer
trending_skills.description: Audit OpenClaw token usage, purge stale sessions, and optimize inference speed. If you run multiple agents, this shows you where tokens are burning.
trending_skills.why_trending: New this week. 29 downloads. With Opus 4.6 pricing, token awareness pays for itself fast.

trending_skills.name: Shop Product Search
trending_skills.description: Search, compare, and buy products from millions of online stores. No API keys needed.
trending_skills.why_trending: 31 downloads in 2 days. Zero-config shopping agent — useful for price comparisons and deal hunting.

trending_skills.name: Security Audit Tools
trending_skills.description: Inspect third-party Claude/OpenClaw/Codex skills, plugins, repos, and npm packages for malicious patterns.
trending_skills.why_trending: Post-ClawHavoc essential. Scans SKILL.md files, npm deps, and shell commands for red flags.

ai_tweets.headline: Block laid off 4,000 workers after deploying AI agent internally
ai_tweets.detail: Block Inc. (fka Square) used its internal AI agent 'Goose' to automate workflows and condense production timelines — then cut 4,000 jobs. Morgan Stanley flagged this as the first major case of AI agents directly enabling workforce reduction at scale.
ai_tweets.takeaway: The 'agents replace tasks not people' narrative just got harder to defend. If you're building agents for companies, the displacement question is now unavoidable.

ai_tweets.headline: OpenClaw crosses 250,000 GitHub stars
ai_tweets.detail: Wikipedia updated. 100K+ active users. The ecosystem is no longer early-adopter — it's mainstream developer tooling.
ai_tweets.takeaway: Platform risk is dropping. More stars = more contributors = faster patches = safer to build on.

ai_tweets.headline: AI inference is now 'critical infrastructure' — secure it accordingly
ai_tweets.detail: Panel at Quantum Insider: agentic identities, model extraction, prompt injection, and data drift are converging. Treat AI inference like you treat servers — inventory, access control, monitoring.
ai_tweets.takeaway: If your agent has access to email, calendar, and files — it IS infrastructure. Lock it down.

prompt_hack.title: Token Budget Awareness
steps: Run a token audit on your workspace: 'Audit my token usage for the last 7 days'
Identify which cron jobs or heartbeats burn the most tokens
Switch high-volume, low-stakes tasks to Sonnet; keep Opus for strategic work
prompt_hack.why_it_matters: Most agent setups waste 30-50% of tokens on routine checks that don't need top-tier models. One audit can cut your bill significantly.

model_radar.detail: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B is a mixture-of-experts model — 35B total params but only 3B active per token. Runs on consumer GPUs. Dominating HuggingFace trending with 900+ likes in a week.
model_radar.relevance: If you want a capable local model for drafts, brainstorming, or high-volume tasks without API costs, this is the new king. Already has GGUF quantizations via Unsloth.

model_radar.detail: Released yesterday. PDF analysis tool, speech-to-text API, SecretRef for 64 targets, 150+ fixes across 93 contributors. Biggest release of the year so far.
model_radar.relevance: We're on v2026.3.1 — worth updating. The PDF tool alone is worth it if you process documents.

community_build.title: Build & Sell Niche Automation with OpenClaw
community_build.description: QuantoSei published a deep guide on treating OpenClaw skills as micro-products. The thesis: find a niche workflow (real estate, trading cards, property management), wrap it in a skill, publish to ClawHub. PropClaw just did this — full property management for US landlords with double-entry GL and FCRA screening. 18 downloads in day one.
community_build.how_to_replicate: Pick a domain you know. Map the 5-10 most repetitive tasks. Build a skill that handles them. Publish. The ClawHub marketplace is still early enough that good niche skills get discovered organically.
